mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.5 percent within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate revenue growth materializes.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of disinflation combined with stable short-term rates, real yields decline while nominal volatility drops, expanding the equity risk premium and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains flat over a 2-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate directly increases aggregate corporate revenues, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth relative to 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales and food services exceeding 5 percent signals strong consumer demand momentum, which lifts cyclical equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months where RSAFS growth exceeds the 5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market normalization, which reduces recession probabilities and compresses credit spreads, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE predicts negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production accelerates while consumer price inflation remains contained, supply-side efficiency gains expand corporate profit margins, which mechanically lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5 percent monthly but CPIAUCSL growth simultaneously exceeds 0.3 percent monthly.
